items.description: The head of the U.N. children's agency visited northwestern Nigeria on Friday as conflict, poverty and rising food insecurity leave millions of children in the region in need of aid

items.title: Spain won the World Cup, but Donald Trump 'cast a shadow'
items.description: Though Donald Trump mostly stayed clear of stadiums, the World Cup may influence the US midterm elections. DW looks at the political implications of "the most successful sporting event maybe in the history of the world."

items.title: South Africa says radioactive contamination at nuclear plant was minor
items.description: Energy Minister Kgosientsho Ramokgopa said the incidents were handled efficiently and reinforced confidence in the management of the plant and South Africa's capacity to move into a new phase of nuclear generation

items.title: Guyana: Dozens feared dead after ferry capsizes
items.description: Rescuers have been able to save 67 passengers so far. It is unknown exactly how many people were on board as authorities said the passengers' list is inaccurate.

items.title: Ethiopians march against alleged forced mobilisation in Tigray
items.description: Thousands rally in Ethiopia's capital Addis Ababa to protest the alleged forced recruitment of civilians in the Tigray region by Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) forces, the breakaway region's main political party, amid months of escalating tensions with Ethiopia's federal government

items.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
items.description: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.

items.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
items.description: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

items.title: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye elected new Chairman of ECOWAS
items.description: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye is the new Chairman of ECOWAS. The 46-year-old was elected during the regional bloc's summit in Sierra Leone, taking over from President Julius Maada Bio.

items.title: Milei says won't attend World Cup final out of superstition
items.description: British calls on FIFA to investigate after Argentina's players held up a banner reading "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" after their victory over England. Leer más

items.title: Nigeria and Ghana call on AU to address 'Afrophobic protests' in South Africa
items.description: Nigeria and Ghana have condemned what they called the recent "Afrophobic protests targeting African nationals in South Africa." In a statement, Nigeria's ministry of foreign affairs said the two countries want the issue placed on the agenda at the next African Union summit.

items.title: Senate debate on ruling party’s land ownership bill pushed back after spat
items.description: Milei government fails to gather votes, postpones upper house debate over land ownership reform bill; Fourth delay for controversial bill that would eliminate restrictions on the foreign purchase of rural land; Comes after spat between Vice-President Villarruel and La Libertad Avanza Senate chief Patricia Bullrich. Leer más

items.title: Oil settles 1% higher as hopes of renewed US-Iran negotiations offset Houthi threat
What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.description: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.
